query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM created_at) = 2025;
fix: Replaced EXTRACT with range comparison: WHERE created_at >= '2025-01-01' AND created_at < '2026-01-01'. Listed specific columns instead of SELECT *.

query: SELECT id, name FROM customers WHERE LOWER(email) = 'john@example.com';
fix: Added a generated column or functional index on LOWER(email). Alternatively, normalized email to lowercase on insert so the function is unnecessary at query time.
Used a case-insensitive collation or citext column type. Replaced SELECT * with specific columns needed.

query: SELECT * FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id;
fix: Listed only needed columns: c.name, o.order_date, oi.product_name, oi.quantity. Reduced network transfer and allowed covering index usage.
Added WHERE clause with date filter: DELETE FROM session_logs WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'. For full table wipes, use TRUNCATE instead.
Added WHERE clause to target specific rows: UPDATE products SET is_active = false WHERE last_sold_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '1 year'.

query: SELECT id, name FROM customers WHERE name LIKE '%enterprise%';
fix: Implemented full-text search using tsvector/tsquery (PostgreSQL) or FULLTEXT index (MySQL). For simple prefix matches, reversed to 'enterprise%' where possible.

query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%@gmail.com';
fix: Added a separate email_domain column populated on insert. Query becomes WHERE email_domain = 'gmail.com' with an index on email_domain.

query: SELECT o.id, o.total FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id WHERE c.country = 'US' ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;
fix: Added LIMIT 100 to prevent unbounded result sets. Verified index exists on orders.customer_id (foreign key) and customers.country.

query: SELECT c.name, o.total FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed';
fix: Moved the filter into the ON clause: LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'completed'. This preserves non-matching customer rows.

query: SELECT e.name, d.dept_name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id WHERE d.active = true;
fix: Changed to INNER JOIN since filtering on the right table negates the LEFT. If LEFT JOIN behavior is needed, moved filter to ON clause.

query: SELECT id, name, revenue FROM companies ORDER BY revenue DESC;
fix: Added LIMIT 50 to return only top results. For pagination, added LIMIT with OFFSET or keyset pagination.

query: SELECT id FROM customers WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL);
fix: Replaced NOT IN with NOT EXISTS: WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders WHERE orders.customer_id = customers.id). Handles NULLs correctly and often faster.

query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics' OR brand = 'Apple';
fix: Split into two queries with UNION ALL for better index usage: SELECT id FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics' UNION ALL SELECT id FROM products WHERE brand = 'Apple'.

query: SELECT e.name, (SELECT AVG(s.amount) FROM sales s WHERE s.employee_id = e.id) as avg_sale FROM employees e ORDER BY avg_sale DESC;
fix: Replaced correlated subquery with JOIN: SELECT e.name, AVG(s.amount) FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.id = s.employee_id GROUP BY e.name ORDER BY avg_sale DESC LIMIT 20. Executes once instead of per-row.
